institutional_unit: Health systems deploying the studied population-health risk algorithm
decision_process: Selection of patients for high-risk care-management resources using an algorithmic risk score
sampling_rule: Retrospective assessment of the public record available at the study publication cutoff; the public sources do not disclose a complete deployment-period record.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer, Powers, Vogeli, and Mullainathan, Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations, Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342

requested_missing_artifacts: Reviewer access logs and evidence packets used before a patient was selected or excluded
substantive_findings.rationale: The article establishes the allocation mechanism and disparity but does not disclose what evidence individual enrolling reviewers could access.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer, Powers, Vogeli, and Mullainathan, Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations, Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342

requested_missing_artifacts: Individualized clinical rationales, exception records, or disagreement logs
substantive_findings.rationale: The public record does not distinguish independent clinical judgment from routine acceptance of the risk score.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer, Powers, Vogeli, and Mullainathan, Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations, Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342

requested_missing_artifacts: Authority matrix, override permissions, and exercised override records at deploying institutions
substantive_findings.rationale: No public artifact shows whether a named deployer-side authority could reject, modify, or stop the score-driven allocation path.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer, Powers, Vogeli, and Mullainathan, Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations, Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342

requested_missing_artifacts: Patient notice, appeal register, escalation log, and reversal records
substantive_findings.rationale: The public record does not establish an operative patient or reviewer contestability channel for the allocation decision.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer, Powers, Vogeli, and Mullainathan, Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations, Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342

requested_missing_artifacts: Identification and remediation records for patients under-selected under the original proxy
substantive_findings.rationale: No deployer-side public record shows whether previously affected patients were identified, reviewed, notified, or remediated.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer, Powers, Vogeli, and Mullainathan, Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations, Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342

requested_missing_artifacts: Deploying-institution records revising, suspending, or retiring use of the tool
substantive_findings.rationale: The public record does not establish whether deploying health systems changed their own workflows after publication.

telemetry_integrity.provenance: The peer-reviewed article is traceable, but deployer-side operational records are not public.
telemetry_integrity.completeness: The public record is rich on model effects and sparse on institutional oversight artifacts.
telemetry_integrity.edit_authority: The public sources do not identify who controlled or could modify deployer-side decision records.
telemetry_integrity.independence: No independent deployer-side audit record is available.
telemetry_integrity.tamper_evidence: No public tamper-evidence controls are documented for the deployment records.
telemetry_integrity.retention: No public retention rule is documented for the relevant allocation records.
telemetry_integrity.rationale: The evidence supports analysis of the algorithmic mechanism but is insufficient to assess the integrity of deployer-side oversight records.

summary: Every deployer-side substantive dimension returns insufficient evidence; the absence of public oversight records is itself the central finding.
limitations: The period fields represent the public evidence cutoff because deployment start and end dates are not disclosed in the reviewed sources.
The assessment does not infer absence of oversight from nonpublication.
The studied algorithm influenced allocation to additional care-management resources; this assessment does not characterize it as a direct denial-of-care system.
